---
title: "Tools & Software for AI/ML Development"
subtitle: "Essential software, libraries, and platforms for your AI journey"
format: html
---

# Programming Languages

## Python: The Lingua Franca of AI

Python is the dominant language in AI/ML due to its simplicity and rich ecosystem.

### Getting Started
- **Official Python**: [python.org](https://www.python.org)
- **Anaconda Distribution**: [anaconda.com](https://www.anaconda.com) - Includes most ML libraries pre-installed
- **Python Tutorial**: [docs.python.org/3/tutorial](https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial)

### Essential Python Libraries

| Library | Purpose | Installation |
|---------|---------|--------------|
| NumPy | Numerical computing | `pip install numpy` |
| Pandas | Data manipulation | `pip install pandas` |
| Matplotlib | Data visualization | `pip install matplotlib` |
| Scikit-learn | Classical ML | `pip install scikit-learn` |
| TensorFlow | Deep learning | `pip install tensorflow` |
| PyTorch | Deep learning | `pip install torch` |
| Keras | High-level neural networks API | `pip install keras` |

---

# Development Environments

## Jupyter Ecosystem

### Jupyter Notebook
The classic interactive computing environment.
- **Website**: [jupyter.org](https://jupyter.org)
- **Install**: `pip install jupyter`
- **Launch**: `jupyter notebook`

### JupyterLab
Next-generation interface with tabs, terminals, and more.
- **Install**: `pip install jupyterlab`
- **Launch**: `jupyter lab`

### Google Colab
Free cloud-based Jupyter environment with GPU/TPU access.
- **Website**: [colab.research.google.com](https://colab.research.google.com)
- **Pros**: Free GPU, no setup required, easy sharing
- **Cons**: Session timeouts, limited compute hours

### Kaggle Kernels
Similar to Colab, integrated with Kaggle datasets and competitions.
- **Website**: [kaggle.com/kernels](https://www.kaggle.com/kernels)

## IDEs and Code Editors

### Visual Studio Code
Highly recommended for Python development.
- **Website**: [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com)
- **Essential Extensions**:
  - Python (Microsoft)
  - Jupyter (Microsoft)
  - Pylance (Microsoft)
  - GitLens (GitKraken)

### PyCharm
Full-featured Python IDE by JetBrains.
- **Community Edition**: Free
- **Professional Edition**: Paid (free for students)
- **Website**: [jetbrains.com/pycharm](https://www.jetbrains.com/pycharm)

---

# Machine Learning Frameworks

## Classical Machine Learning

### Scikit-learn
The go-to library for classical ML algorithms.
- **Website**: [scikit-learn.org](https://scikit-learn.org)
- **Best for**: Regression, classification, clustering, dimensionality reduction
- **Documentation**: Excellent with many examples

### XGBoost
Gradient boosting library known for winning Kaggle competitions.
- **Website**: [xgboost.ai](https://xgboost.ai)
- **Install**: `pip install xgboost`

### LightGBM
Microsoft's gradient boosting framework, faster than XGBoost.
- **Website**: [lightgbm.readthedocs.io](https://lightgbm.readthedocs.io)
- **Install**: `pip install lightgbm`

## Deep Learning

### TensorFlow & Keras
Google's end-to-end ML platform.
- **Website**: [tensorflow.org](https://www.tensorflow.org)
- **Best for**: Production deployment, mobile/edge ML
- **Keras**: High-level API included with TensorFlow

### PyTorch
Facebook's dynamic neural network library.
- **Website**: [pytorch.org](https://pytorch.org)
- **Best for**: Research, flexibility, NLP
- **Growing adoption** in both academia and industry

### JAX
Google's library for high-performance numerical computing.
- **Website**: [github.com/google/jax](https://github.com/google/jax)
- **Best for**: Research requiring automatic differentiation

## Natural Language Processing

### Hugging Face Transformers
The standard library for NLP with pre-trained models.
- **Website**: [huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co)
- **Install**: `pip install transformers`
- **Features**: BERT, GPT, T5, and thousands of other models

### spaCy
Industrial-strength NLP library.
- **Website**: [spacy.io](https://spacy.io)
- **Best for**: Production NLP pipelines
- **Install**: `pip install spacy`

### NLTK
Educational and research-oriented NLP library.
- **Website**: [nltk.org](https://www.nltk.org)
- **Best for**: Learning NLP concepts
- **Install**: `pip install nltk`

## Computer Vision

### OpenCV
Comprehensive computer vision library.
- **Website**: [opencv.org](https://opencv.org)
- **Install**: `pip install opencv-python`

### Pillow
Python Imaging Library.
- **Website**: [python-pillow.org](https://python-pillow.org)
- **Install**: `pip install Pillow`

---

# Deployment Tools

## Web Frameworks for ML

### Streamlit
The fastest way to build ML web apps.
- **Website**: [streamlit.io](https://streamlit.io)
- **Install**: `pip install streamlit`
- **Best for**: Quick prototypes, demos, internal tools

### Gradio
Create ML demos with just a few lines of code.
- **Website**: [gradio.app](https://gradio.app)
- **Install**: `pip install gradio`
- **Best for**: Sharing models, quick demos

### FastAPI
Modern, fast web framework for building APIs.
- **Website**: [fastapi.tiangolo.com](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com)
- **Install**: `pip install fastapi uvicorn`
- **Best for**: Production ML APIs

### Flask
Lightweight web framework.
- **Website**: [flask.palletsprojects.com](https://flask.palletsprojects.com)
- **Install**: `pip install flask`

## Containerization

### Docker
Package applications with all dependencies.
- **Website**: [docker.com](https://www.docker.com)
- **Essential for**: Reproducible deployments

## Cloud Platforms

### Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
- **AI Platform**: Managed ML service
- **Vertex AI**: Unified ML platform
- **Free Tier**: $300 credit for new users

### Amazon Web Services (AWS)
- **SageMaker**: End-to-end ML platform
- **Free Tier**: 12 months free for certain services

### Microsoft Azure
- **Azure ML**: Cloud-based ML service
- **Free Tier**: $200 credit for new users

---

# Version Control & Collaboration

## Git & GitHub

### Git
Version control system essential for all developers.
- **Website**: [git-scm.com](https://git-scm.com)
- **Learn**: [git-scm.com/book](https://git-scm.com/book)

### GitHub
Platform for hosting and collaborating on code.
- **Website**: [github.com](https://github.com)
- **Student Developer Pack**: Free premium tools for students

### GitLab
Alternative to GitHub with built-in CI/CD.
- **Website**: [gitlab.com](https://gitlab.com)

## DVC (Data Version Control)
Version control for datasets and models.
- **Website**: [dvc.org](https://dvc.org)
- **Install**: `pip install dvc`

---

# Experiment Tracking & Monitoring

## Experiment Tracking

### Weights & Biases (W&B)
Industry-standard experiment tracking.
- **Website**: [wandb.ai](https://wandb.ai)
- **Free Tier**: Available for students and open source
- **Install**: `pip install wandb`

### MLflow
Open-source platform for ML lifecycle management.
- **Website**: [mlflow.org](https://mlflow.org)
- **Install**: `pip install mlflow`

### TensorBoard
Visualization toolkit for TensorFlow (also works with PyTorch).
- **Website**: [tensorflow.org/tensorboard](https://www.tensorflow.org/tensorboard)
- **Install**: `pip install tensorboard`

## Model Monitoring

### Evidently AI
Open-source monitoring for ML models.
- **Website**: [evidentlyai.com](https://www.evidentlyai.com)
- **Best for**: Drift detection, data quality

---

# Data Tools

## Data Collection

### Kaggle API
Download datasets programmatically.
- **Install**: `pip install kaggle`

### Google Dataset Search
Search engine for datasets.
- **Website**: [datasetsearch.research.google.com](https://datasetsearch.research.google.com)

## Data Processing

### Apache Spark
Distributed computing framework for big data.
- **Website**: [spark.apache.org](https://spark.apache.org)
- **PySpark**: Python API for Spark

### Dask
Parallel computing library that scales Python code.
- **Website**: [dask.org](https://dask.org)
- **Install**: `pip install dask`

## Data Visualization

### Plotly
Interactive visualization library.
- **Website**: [plotly.com](https://plotly.com)
- **Install**: `pip install plotly`

### Seaborn
Statistical data visualization based on matplotlib.
- **Website**: [seaborn.pydata.org](https://seaborn.pydata.org)
- **Install**: `pip install seaborn`

---

# Learning Resources Built with These Tools

## Interactive Tutorials

1. **Kaggle Learn**
   - Micro-courses on ML topics
   - Hands-on coding exercises
   - [kaggle.com/learn](https://www.kaggle.com/learn)

2. **Google Colab Examples**
   - Ready-to-run notebooks
   - No setup required
   - [colab.research.google.com/github](https://colab.research.google.com/github)

3. **Hugging Face Course**
   - Free NLP course
   - Hands-on with transformers
   - [huggingface.co/course](https://huggingface.co/course)

## Template Projects

### Cookiecutter Data Science
Standardized project structure.
- **Website**: [drivendata.github.io/cookiecutter-data-science](https://drivendata.github.io/cookiecutter-data-science)

### FastAI Course Templates
Templates for deep learning projects.
- **Website**: [course.fast.ai](https://course.fast.ai)

---

# Setting Up Your Development Environment

## Recommended Setup for Beginners

1. **Install Anaconda**
   ```bash
   # Download from anaconda.com
   # Install and create environment
   conda create -n ai-course python=3.9
   conda activate ai-course
   ```

2. **Install Essential Packages**
   ```bash
   pip install jupyterlab numpy pandas matplotlib scikit-learn
   pip install tensorflow  # or pip install torch
   pip install streamlit
   ```

3. **Set Up Git**
   ```bash
   git config --global user.name "Your Name"
   git config --global user.email "your.email@example.com"
   ```

4. **Create GitHub Account**
   - Go to [github.com](https://github.com)
   - Sign up for free account
   - Apply for Student Developer Pack if eligible

## Recommended Setup for Advanced Users

1. **Use PyTorch Lightning** for cleaner deep learning code
2. **Set up Docker** for reproducible environments
3. **Use Weights & Biases** for experiment tracking
4. **Deploy with FastAPI** for production APIs
5. **Use DVC** for data version control

---

# Quick Reference: Installation Commands

```bash
# Core ML stack
pip install numpy pandas matplotlib seaborn scikit-learn

# Deep learning (choose one)
pip install tensorflow  # TensorFlow + Keras
pip install torch torchvision torchaudio  # PyTorch

# NLP
pip install transformers spacy nltk

# Computer Vision
pip install opencv-python pillow

# Web frameworks
pip install streamlit gradio fastapi uvicorn flask

# Experiment tracking
pip install wandb mlflow tensorboard

# Utilities
pip install jupyterlab jupyterlab-git python-dotenv
```

---

# Final Recommendations

## For This Course

1. **Primary Environment**: Google Colab (no setup required)
2. **Local Development**: Anaconda + JupyterLab + VS Code
3. **Version Control**: GitHub
4. **Deployment**: Streamlit Cloud (free)

## Beyond This Course

1. **Specialize**: Choose between TensorFlow or PyTorch based on your goals
2. **Build Portfolio**: Create GitHub repository with projects
3. **Contribute**: Participate in open-source projects
4. **Stay Updated**: Follow blogs, papers, and community discussions

---

# Troubleshooting

## Common Issues

### "Module not found" errors
```bash
# Make sure you're in the right environment
conda activate ai-course  # or your environment name
pip install package-name
```

### GPU not detected
```bash
# For TensorFlow
pip install tensorflow-gpu

# For PyTorch, install from pytorch.org with CUDA support
```

### Permission errors on Linux/Mac
```bash
# Use --user flag or virtual environment
pip install --user package-name
# Or better: use conda/virtualenv
```

## Getting Help

1. **Stack Overflow**: Tag your question with specific library names
2. **GitHub Issues**: Report bugs and ask questions on project repositories
3. **Community Forums**: Reddit r/MachineLearning, Kaggle Forums
4. **Discord/Slack**: Many projects have active community channels

---

Happy Coding! 🚀
